top_categories.workout_context: At mile 60 of 131, glycogen is gone. This workout teaches your body to produce watts on fumes.
Race-pace efforts that mimic L'Eroica's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before October.
12,707ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
97ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of L'Eroica. Train it until it feels boring.
L'Eroica's tuscan hills — strade bianche forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 131 miles.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 12,707ft of climbing on tuscan hills — strade bianche does to your power file on race day.
At L'Eroica, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
131 miles rewards fat oxidation. This volume work shifts your fuel mix so you're still burning fat at hour 5, not bonking at hour 3.
Every hard session works better with a bigger aerobic base. At 131 miles, base fitness is the difference between racing and surviving.
Short, max efforts are unavoidable. Punchy climbs, gap closures, attacks on tuscan hills — strade bianche. If you haven't trained them, you'll crack.
Two threshold sessions per week without the recovery cost of VO2max work. For 131 miles, sustainable FTP gains matter more than peak power.

race_overlay.nutrition: Target 60–80g carbs/hour for L'Eroica’s 131 miles. Start fueling within the first 30 minutes — early fueling prevents late-race collapse. Bonking at mile 60 is a nutrition failure, not a fitness failure.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, Race Simulation, and VO2max to prepare you for 131 miles of tuscan hills — strade bianche in Gaiole in Chianti, Siena, Tuscany.
